{"question":"Which files define the API route layer and the auth middleware?","answer":"The API route stack lives in packages/server/src/routes/api.ts and it applies auth middleware exported from packages/server/src/middleware/index.ts, which points at auth-guard.ts.","evidence_paths":["packages/server/src/routes/api.ts","packages/server/src/middleware/index.ts","packages/server/src/middleware/auth-guard.ts"],"next_steps":["Summarize the repo goal from README.md."]}

{"question":"What does the repo README say the project is for?","answer":"The repo is a compact TypeScript auth API demo with route wiring, middleware, and request-scoped user context.","evidence_paths":["README.md"],"next_steps":[]}

{"ready":true,"plan_summary":"Enough context gathered to answer directly.","final_response":"This repo is a small TypeScript auth API demo. Requests enter through packages/server/src/routes/api.ts, flow through middleware exported by packages/server/src/middleware/index.ts, and the auth check itself lives in packages/server/src/middleware/auth-guard.ts. The auth guard reads the API key header, validates it against an in-memory key table, and attaches a user object to the request context.","success_criteria":["Explain the repo in simple terms.","Name the key auth and API files."],"questions":[],"actions":[]}
